title: Holcus tenuis
taxonomy: Holcus tenuis, also known as slender soft grass or Yorkshire fog, is classified under the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and is further grouped into the subfamily Pooideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the Poaceae family. The genus Holcus is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which comprises of various grass species. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and taxonomic position of Holcus tenuis within the plant kingdom.

benefits: Holcus tenuis, also known as slender soft brome, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties.
